ooabap-027.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Real-time ticket matlab woh actual production problem jo client ne raise kiya aur tumhne solve kiya. Imagine karo ek bade retail company ka SAP system — raat ko batch job chala, subah user ne report khola aur data galat tha. Ticket raise hua: 'Factory pattern se ek wrong object create ho raha hai, pricing galat calculate ho rahi hai.' Yeh OOABAP aur Design Pattern ka real ticket hai. Ek aur example: ek Observer pattern implement hua tha jisme multiple output channels (email, PDF, EDI) ek order save event pe trigger hote the — ek channel fail ho raha tha aur baaki bhi fail ho raha tha kyunki exception handling nahi tha. Aur teesra common ticket: singleton class ka shared state corrupt ho gaya tha kyunki ek method ne internal buffer reset kar diya tha bina doosri jagah se coordinate kiye. Ye teeno tickets OOABAP aur design patterns ki galat implementation se aate hain aur ek senior ABAP developer se expect kiya jaata hai ki woh inhe diagnose aur fix kare.
ooabap-027.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Real-time ticket interview mein poochha jaata hai yeh check karne ke liye ki tumhare paas actual hands-on experience hai ya sirf theory. Teen real scenarios detail mein samjho:

Ticket 1 — Factory Pattern: Wrong Object Creation:
Client ka pricing module crash kar raha tha certain company codes ke liye. Root cause: factory method mein ek CASE statement tha jo company code se correct pricing strategy class select karta tha. Naya company code add hua tha customizing mein lekin factory method update nahi kiya gaya tha. Factory ne default case pe ek NULL reference return kiya, caller ne bina NULL check kiye method call ki — CX_SY_REF_IS_INITIAL dump. Fix: factory mein default ELSE mein exception raise karo (ZCX_UNKNOWN_COMPANY_CODE) instead of returning initial reference, aur caller mein TRY-CATCH add karo. Improvement: factory ko database-driven banao — hard-coded CASE ki jagah configuration table se class name read karo.

Ticket 2 — Observer Pattern: Exception Propagation Bug:
Order save pe multiple observers notify hote the — email sender, EDI generator, audit logger. Ek raat EDI server down tha, EDI generator ne exception throw ki. Subject class mein LOOP AT observers tha aur ek observer ki exception ne poora loop terminate kar diya — email aur audit log bhi nahi hue. Fix: LOOP ke andar TRY-CATCH — ek observer fail ho toh doosre continue karein. Aur failed observer ka error log karo ek separate error table mein. Ye Observer pattern ka critical design consideration hai.

Ticket 3 — Singleton: Shared State Corruption:
Configuration singleton class tha jo company-specific parameters cache karta tha. Ek background job alag company code ke liye kaam karta tha aur singleton ke REFRESH method ko call karta tha — is se poora cache clear ho jaata tha. Doosra concurrent job jo online transaction serve kar raha tha use stale data milta tha jab woh cache reload karta tha. Fix: singleton ko company code aware banao — dictionary (HASHED TABLE) se keyed on company code. Har company code ka alag cache — refresh ek company code ka doosri ko affect nahi karta.
ooabap-027.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'I have solved multiple OOABAP and design pattern related tickets. The most impactful was a Factory pattern issue where a new company code was added in configuration but the factory method had a hard-coded CASE statement that returned an initial reference for unknown codes. The caller did not null-check the reference before invoking a method, causing a CX_SY_REF_IS_INITIAL dump in production. I fixed the factory to raise ZCX_UNKNOWN_COMPANY_CODE instead of returning null, added TRY-CATCH in the caller, and refactored the factory to be configuration-driven — class names stored in a Z-table instead of hard-coded CASE. A second ticket involved Observer pattern where one observer throwing an unhandled exception terminated the notification loop — I wrapped each observer call in TRY-CATCH so failures are isolated and logged without stopping other observers. A third was a Singleton with shared mutable state corrupted by concurrent processes — I refactored the cache to be keyed by company code so each process works with isolated state.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein teen cheezein clearly bolo — problem kya tha, root cause kya tha, fix kya kiya. 'Ticket resolve kiya' bolna kafi nahi — technical depth dikhao. Ye answer interviewer ko proof deta hai ki tumne actual OOABAP code debug kiya hai.
ooabap-027.codeExamples:
" Ticket fix: Factory returns exception instead of null
CLASS zcl_pricing_factory DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS create
      IMPORTING iv_bukrs        TYPE bukrs
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_strat) TYPE REF TO zif_pricing_strategy
      RAISING   zcx_unknown_company.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_pricing_factory IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD create.
    " Config-driven: read class name from Z-table
    SELECT SINGLE class_name FROM zprice_config
      WHERE bukrs = @iv_bukrs INTO @DATA(lv_class).
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_unknown_company
        EXPORTING iv_bukrs = iv_bukrs.
    ENDIF.
    TRY.
      CREATE OBJECT ro_strat TYPE (lv_class).
    CATCH cx_sy_create_object_error.
      RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_unknown_company
        EXPORTING iv_bukrs = iv_bukrs.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

ooabap-027.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Real production ticket — pricing factory silently returned initial reference for new company code; fix is config-driven factory plus typed exception.

Used for:
- Story-style interview answer about a P1 ticket
- Showing config-table dispatch pattern (ZPRICE_CONFIG)
- Defensive dynamic CREATE OBJECT with CX_SY_CREATE_OBJECT_ERROR

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- SELECT SINGLE from ZPRICE_CONFIG keyed by BUKRS
- CREATE OBJECT ro_strat TYPE (lv_class) — dynamic dispatch
- RAISING zcx_unknown_company with EXPORTING context
- SE24 to edit factory, SE38 ATC pre-transport, ABAP Unit added
- ST22 to confirm dump root cause; SM20 for who triggered it

Interview keyword: Config-driven factory + typed exception = no silent fall-through.

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you identify the root cause of the Factory pattern null reference dump?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ST22 ne factory return value par method call par CX_SY_REF_IS_INITIAL dikhaya. Maine factory tak trace kiya — CASE statement mein new company code ke liye koi entry nahi thi aur koi ELSE clause nahi thi, isliye returning reference kabhi set nahi hua. Caller ne assume kiya ki factory hamesha valid object return karta hai. Root cause dual tha: factory mein unknown inputs ke liye koi defensive handling nahi thi, aur caller ke paas koi null guard nahi tha. Fix ne dono sides address kiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ST22 showed CX_SY_REF_IS_INITIAL at the method call on the factory return value. I traced back to the factory — the CASE statement had no entry for the new company code and no ELSE clause, so the returning reference was never set. The caller assumed the factory always returned a valid object. Root cause was dual: factory had no defensive handling for unknown inputs, and the caller had no null guard. Fix addressed both sides.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Ye ek bahut common OOABAP production issue hai. Factory mein ELSE branch add karo aur caller mein IS BOUND check ya exception handling. Double defense: factory raise kare, caller catch kare.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf caller mein IS BOUND check add karna without fixing the factory. Factory should never return null — it should raise an exception. Both fixes are needed.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Caller side fix
TRY.
  DATA(lo_strat) = zcl_pricing_factory=>create( iv_bukrs = lv_bukrs ).
  DATA(lv_price) = lo_strat->calculate( iv_matnr = lv_mat iv_qty = lv_qty ).
CATCH zcx_unknown_company INTO DATA(lx_err).
  MESSAGE |Unknown company { lx_err->mv_bukrs }| TYPE 'E'.
ENDTRY.

followupAnswerBank.question: What was the Observer pattern ticket and how did you fix it?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Subject ke notify method ne registered observers ke through loop karke on_event call kiya. Ek observer ne unhandled exception raise kiya, jo loop ke bahar propagate ho gaya aur poori notification chain ko terminate kar diya. Fix: har observer call ko TRY-CATCH BEFORE UNWIND mein wrap kiya, error ko observer class name aur exception text ke saath Z-error table mein log kiya, aur next observer par jaane ke liye CONTINUE use kiya. Isne notification chain ko individual observer failures ke liye resilient banaya.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The subject's notify method looped through registered observers calling on_event. One observer raised an unhandled exception, which propagated out of the loop and terminated the entire notification chain. Fix: wrapped each observer call in TRY-CATCH BEFORE UNWIND, logged the error with observer class name and exception text to a Z-error table, and used CONTINUE to proceed to the next observer. This made the notification chain resilient to individual observer failures.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: BEFORE UNWIND vs standard CATCH: BEFORE UNWIND mein stack unwind nahi hota — exception propagation roke bina context preserve hota hai for logging. Production Observer implementations mein ye best practice hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Exception ko silently swallow karna bina logging ke — bad observer ki failure invisible ho jaati hai. Hamesha log karo — error table mein ya application log (SLG1).
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
METHOD notify_all_observers.
  LOOP AT mt_observers ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<obs>).
    TRY.
      <obs>->on_event( iv_event = iv_event_type ).
    CATCH cx_root BEFORE UNWIND INTO DATA(lx_err).
      " Log and continue — do not let one failure stop others
      INSERT VALUE #( observer = <obs>->get_name( )
                      error    = lx_err->get_text( )
                      timestamp = sy-uzeit )
             INTO TABLE mt_error_log.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How did you debug the Singleton shared state corruption issue?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Maine dono concurrent processes ke SM37 job logs compare kiye aur paaya ki job A ka singleton REFRESH ka execution time job B ke cache read window ke saath overlap karta tha. Job B ne phir job A ka REFRESH complete hone se pehle stale data load kiya. Fix: singleton cache ko flat buffer se company code ke key wala HASHED TABLE banaya. Har process sirf apni key refresh karta hai — koi cross-process state contamination nahi. Concurrent processes ab mutable state share nahi karte.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I compared SM37 job logs for both concurrent processes and found that job A's execution time for the singleton REFRESH overlapped with job B's cache read window. Job B then loaded stale data before job A's REFRESH completed. Fix: changed the singleton cache from a flat buffer to a HASHED TABLE keyed by company code. Each process refreshes only its key — no cross-process state contamination. Concurrent processes no longer share mutable state.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Concurrency bugs in ABAP: ABAP work processes share memory only via shared objects or cluster databases. Regular class attributes are not shared across processes — but if the singleton was implemented via Shared Objects (CL_ABAP_SHARED_OBJECTS), then cross-process state IS possible. Know your runtime context.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Singleton ko always blame karna for concurrency — identify whether processes actually share memory. Regular CLASS-DATA singletons are per-process, not cross-process.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Keyed cache: no cross-company contamination
CLASS zcl_config_singleton DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA mt_cache TYPE HASHED TABLE OF zconfig_entry
      WITH UNIQUE KEY bukrs.
    CLASS-DATA mo_instance TYPE REF TO zcl_config_singleton.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS get_instance
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_inst) TYPE REF TO zcl_config_singleton.
    METHODS get_config
      IMPORTING iv_bukrs        TYPE bukrs
      RETURNING VALUE(rs_config) TYPE zconfig_entry.
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you prevent Factory pattern issues in future development?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Factory issues ke liye prevention strategy: factory configuration-driven banao ek Z-table use karke taaki new types add karne ke liye kabhi code change ki zaroorat na ho. Ek strict contract enforce karo — factory unknown inputs ke liye typed exception raise karta hai, kabhi initial reference return nahi karta. Negative cases ke liye unit tests likho exception ko explicitly test karte hue. Code review mein, verify karo ki saare factory return value usages ke paas null checks ya TRY-CATCH blocks hain. ATC custom check unguarded factory calls ko flag kar sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Prevention strategy for factory issues: make the factory configuration-driven using a Z-table so adding new types never requires code change. Enforce a strict contract — factory raises a typed exception for unknown inputs, never returns initial reference. Write unit tests for negative cases explicitly testing the exception. In code review, verify all factory return value usages have null checks or TRY-CATCH blocks. ATC custom check can flag unguarded factory calls.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Configuration-driven factory is the gold standard — zero code change when business adds new variants. Store class name as string in Z-table, use CREATE OBJECT TYPE (lv_class_name) with exception handling for invalid class names.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Hard-coded factory ki CASE statement ko baar baar update karna. Every time a new business variant is added, a transport is needed. Config-driven factory eliminates this recurring risk.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Z-table: ZPRICE_CONFIG (bukrs, class_name, active)
" Adding a new company = table entry, no code transport
SELECT SINGLE class_name FROM zprice_config
  WHERE bukrs = @iv_bukrs AND active = 'X'
  INTO @DATA(lv_class).
IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR lv_class IS INITIAL.
  RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_factory_config_missing
    EXPORTING iv_bukrs = iv_bukrs.
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What ABAP transactions did you use to resolve these tickets?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Factory dump ke liye: ST22 ne exact exception, program, aur line number diya — directly bug tak. Observer ticket ke liye: SLG1 application log ne dikhaya ki failure se pehle kaunse observers successful huye. Singleton concurrency ke liye: timestamps wale SM37 job logs ne overlap reveal kiya. Fixes ke baad, class changes verify karne ke liye SE24, unit tests pass karne ke liye ATC ensure karne ke liye, phir transport ke liye STMS. Complete resolution ne ST22 — code change — ATC — STMS pipeline liya.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For the Factory dump: ST22 gave exact exception, program, and line number — direct to the bug. For the Observer ticket: SLG1 application log showed which observers succeeded before the failure. For Singleton concurrency: SM37 job logs with timestamps revealed the overlap. After fixes, SE24 to verify class changes, ATC to ensure unit tests pass, then STMS for transport. Complete resolution took ST22 — code change — ATC — STMS pipeline.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: ST22 is always first step for dumps. SLG1 is first step for logic issues without dump. SM37 is first step for batch issues. Ye sequence yaad rakho — interview mein bhi aur production support mein bhi.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Directly code mein jaana bina ST22 ya SLG1 dekhe — guess-based debugging hai, time waste hota hai. Always start with evidence, then trace.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Diagnostic sequence in code (application log)
DATA(lo_log) = NEW cl_bali_log( ).
lo_log->add_message( cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_sy( ) ).
cl_bali_log_db=>save_log_2nd_db_connection(
  io_log          = lo_log
  iv_object       = 'ZPRICE'
  iv_subobject    = 'FACTORY'
  iv_external_id  = iv_bukrs ).

followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Maine resolution ko business terms mein communicate kiya: explain kiya ki kaunsi transactions affected thin (ST22 aur user reports se identified), root cause non-technical language mein — ek system configuration update ek corresponding code update ke saath match nahi hua tha — aur fix ensure karta hai ki future configuration changes manual code updates ke bina automatically handle ho. Maine ab supported saare company codes ki list bhi provide ki aur ticket close karne se pehle UAT ke through client team ke saath confirm kiya.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I communicated the resolution in business terms: explained which transactions were affected (identified from ST22 and user reports), the root cause in non-technical language — a system configuration update was not matched by a corresponding code update — and the fix ensures future configuration changes are automatically handled without manual code updates. I also provided a list of all company codes now supported and confirmed with the client team via UAT before closing the ticket.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Client communication skills are part of ABAP developer role in S/4HANA projects. Technical depth + communication = complete professional. Interview mein ye angle poochha jaata hai especially for senior roles.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Ticket resolution ke baad client ko technical jargon mein explain karna. Client ko result chahiye aur assurance — not factory pattern theory.

" IMPACT: 23 sales orders incorrect pricing

followupAnswerBank.question: What design pattern anti-patterns did you identify in the tickets?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Teen clear anti-patterns emerge huye. Brittle Factory: hard-coded CASE Open-Closed Principle violate karta hai — factory ko modification ke bina extension ke liye open hona chahiye. Fragile Observer: observer chain ke across exception propagation Single Responsibility violate karta hai — subject ko decide nahi karna chahiye ki har observer ka failure doosron ko kaise affect karta hai. Leaky Singleton: bina isolation key ke global mutable state ek thread-safety aur process-safety anti-pattern hai. Ticket ke dauraan in anti-patterns ko recognize aur name karna architectural awareness dikhata hai, sirf bug-fixing nahi.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Three clear anti-patterns emerged. Brittle Factory: hard-coded CASE violates Open-Closed Principle — factory should be open for extension without modification. Fragile Observer: exception propagation across observer chain violates Single Responsibility — the subject should not decide how each observer's failure affects others. Leaky Singleton: global mutable state without isolation key is a thread-safety and process-safety anti-pattern. Recognizing and naming these anti-patterns during the ticket shows architectural awareness, not just bug-fixing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Anti-pattern vocabulary interviewer ko impress karta hai. 'Brittle Factory', 'Fragile Observer', 'Leaky Singleton' — ye terms show karte hain ki tumhara thinking design level pe hai, not just line-by-line code.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Ticket solve kar ke chupchap close karna bina root cause documenting kiye. Anti-pattern identification aur documentation future similar tickets prevent karta hai aur team ke liye learning create karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Anti-pattern: Brittle Factory (CASE-based)
METHOD create.  " BAD - must change code for every new company
  ro_strat = SWITCH #( iv_bukrs
    WHEN '1000' THEN NEW zcl_germany_pricing( )
    WHEN '2000' THEN NEW zcl_india_pricing( )
    WHEN '3000' THEN NEW zcl_us_pricing( ) ).
  " Missing: OTHERS clause -> null returned silently
ENDMETHOD.
" Fix: Config table + dynamic class instantiation

Observer ticket ne audit log mein 8-ghante ka gap cause kiya jisse compliance risk bana. Singleton ticket ne ek full batch pricing job ko wrong output produce karne ka cause kiya jisme re-run ki zaroorat thi. Agle chah mahine ke metrics post-fix ne teeno issue types ki zero recurrence dikhayi. followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Business impact was significant: the Factory ticket caused 23 sales orders with incorrect pricing requiring manual adjustment and credit notes. The Observer ticket caused an 8-hour gap in the audit log creating compliance risk. The Singleton ticket caused a full batch pricing job to produce wrong output requiring re-run. followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Business impact numbers make your story memorable. Interviewers remember '23 orders affected' more than 'there was a pricing bug'. Aur '6 months zero recurrence' shows ki fix effective tha — not just a band-aid.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Business impact bina bolna — 'there was a bug and I fixed it.' This is weak. Always quantify: how many transactions affected, how long it took to fix, what the risk was, what improved after.

ooabap-028.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: OOABAP aur Design Pattern ka issue debug karna regular ABAP debug se thoda alag hai kyunki multiple classes, inheritance chains, aur interface dispatching involve hota hai. Ek example: user bolta hai 'pricing galat aa rahi hai' — tum seedha WRITE statement nahi dekh sakte, kyunki pricing ek interface ke peeche chhupa hai. Pehle kaunsi class actually call ho rahi hai woh dhundho — runtime object ka type check karo. Phir us class ke andar jaao. Design pattern debugging mein specific challenges hain: Factory mein gaya toh kaunsa class create hua? Observer mein gaya toh kaunse observers registered hain? Strategy mein gaya toh kaunsi strategy active hai? ABAP debugger mein in sabka jawab milta hai — runtime type check, object list, method chain trace. Step-by-step approach yaad rakho: (1) Reproduce karo, (2) Entry point dhundho, (3) Object type identify karo at runtime, (4) Method chain follow karo, (5) Data state check karo, (6) Fix identify karo.
ooabap-028.hinglishMasterExplanation:
OOABAP debug karne ki complete methodology samjho:

Step 1 — Reproduce the Issue:
User ke exact inputs lekkar reproduce karo — company code, material number, transaction code, user ID (authorization issue agar ho toh user matters). SE24 ya SE38 se relevant class/program open karo. External breakpoint lagao entry method pe — CLASS-METHODS ya factory entry point.

Step 2 — Runtime Object Type Identification:
Debugger mein variable pe right-click > Show Object Details — runtime type dekho. Interface reference ke peeche kaunsi concrete class hai yeh pata chalta hai. Ya ABAP code mein: DESCRIBE OBJECT lo_ref TYPE DATA(lv_type) se runtime class name pata chalta hai. Is se Factory ne kaunsa object create kiya — confirm ho jaata hai.

Step 3 — Method Call Chain Tracing:
Debugger mein F5 (step into) use karo interface method call pe — ye directly us concrete class ki implementation mein jaata hai. Stack trace dekho debugger ke Call Stack tab mein — poori call chain dikhti hai. Kahan se call aaya, kahan gaya, kaun call kar raha hai — sab clear hota hai.

Step 4 — Internal State Inspection:
Class attributes check karo debugger mein — mv_ aur mo_ prefixed variables. Agar attribute expected value nahi hai — constructor mein break lagao aur check karo ki constructor mein correct values set hue ya nahi. Dependency injection check karo — io_db, io_logger — kya sahi objects inject hue?

Step 5 — Design Pattern Specific Checks:
Factory: CASE statement ya config table entry verify karo. Observer: mt_observers table ki content dekho — registered observers list. Strategy: mo_strategy ka runtime type check karo. Singleton: CLASS-DATA mo_instance bound hai ya nahi, uski state kya hai.

Step 6 — SQL Trace for DB-backed Patterns:
ST05 se SQL trace chalao aur factory/repository calls ke DB queries dekho. Config table se correct entry aa rahi hai? Repository ne correct data fetch kiya? Ye OO patterns ke DB interactions verify karta hai.
ooabap-028.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'My end-to-end debugging approach for OOABAP issues starts with reproducing the issue with exact user inputs and setting a breakpoint at the entry method of the relevant class. In the debugger, I use the Object Details view to identify the runtime type of interface references — this tells me which concrete class the factory created. I follow the method call chain using F5 step-into across interface dispatching to trace execution into the actual implementation. I inspect class attributes to verify internal state was correctly initialized in the constructor. For design pattern specific debugging: factory issues — I check the CASE or config table lookup. Observer issues — I inspect the mt_observers internal table for registered observers. Singleton issues — I verify the CLASS-DATA instance reference and its state. I complement debugger work with ST22 for dump analysis, ST05 for SQL trace on DB-backed patterns, and SLG1 for application log analysis when the issue is non-deterministic.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein step-by-step bolna confidence dikhata hai. 'Pehle reproduce karo, phir runtime type check, phir call chain trace, phir state check' — ye sequence mature debugging mindset hai.
ooabap-028.codeExamples:
" Debug helper: log runtime type at entry point
METHOD debug_runtime_type.
  " Get actual class name of an interface reference
  DATA(lv_class) = cl_abap_classdescr=>describe_by_object_ref(
    p_object_ref = io_strategy )->get_relative_name( ).
  " Log for diagnostic purposes
  MESSAGE |Strategy runtime type: { lv_class }| TYPE 'I'.
ENDMETHOD.

" Debug: dump observer list at runtime
METHOD log_observers.
  LOOP AT mt_observers ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<obs>).
    DATA(lv_obs_class) = cl_abap_classdescr=>describe_by_object_ref(
      p_object_ref = <obs> )->get_relative_name( ).
    cl_bali_log_db=>write_2nd_db_connection(
      io_log_item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create(
        iv_severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_status
        iv_text     = |Observer registered: { lv_obs_class }| ) ).
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

RTTI (Run Time Type Identification) class — describes any ABAP class at runtime.
Factory method jo object reference se class descriptor laata hai.

ooabap-028.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Debugging OOABAP and design patterns — RTTI for runtime type, observer-list inspection, breakpoint placement in dispatched methods.

Used for:
- Identifying which strategy actually ran in a factory dispatch
- Verifying observer registration in Subject classes
- Reading polymorphic call stacks in the ABAP Debugger

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- CL_ABAP_CLASSDESCR=>describe_by_object_ref( )->get_relative_name( )
- CL_BALI_LOG_DB / SLG1 to view application log entries
- ABAP Debugger 'Objects' tab to walk instance attributes
- Conditional breakpoints inside METHOD zif_*~* on relevant key
- ST05 for SQL traces inside methods; SAT for timing

Interview keyword: RTTI tells you who actually ran.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you set breakpoints in OOABAP classes?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main OOABAP mein breakpoints SE24 ke through class implementation open karke aur target line par F9 dabake set karta hoon. Bina restart ke production debugging ke liye, main external breakpoints use karta hoon — code mein BREAK username sirf us user ke liye trigger karta hai. Debugger mein dynamic breakpoints code changes ke bina setting allow karte hain. Interface method debugging ke liye, main breakpoint concrete implementation class mein set karta hoon, interface definition mein nahi, kyunki interface methods mein koi code nahi hota.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I set breakpoints in OOABAP via SE24 by opening the class implementation and pressing F9 on the target line. For production debugging without restarting, I use external breakpoints — BREAK username in code triggers only for that user. Dynamic breakpoints in the debugger allow setting without code changes. For interface method debugging, I set the breakpoint in the concrete implementation class, not the interface definition, because interface methods have no code.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Interface pe breakpoint nahi lagta — sirf implementation class mein lagta hai. Pehle runtime type identify karo (Object Details), phir us concrete class ki implementation mein breakpoint set karo.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Interface definition mein breakpoint lagane ki koshish karna — ye kaam nahi karta kyunki interface method mein code nahi hota. Always break in the implementing class.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" External breakpoint for specific user debugging
METHOD calculate.
  BREAK sy-uname.  " Breaks only for current user
  " Or: BREAK developer_id for specific developer
  DATA(lv_result) = iv_base * mv_rate.
ENDMETHOD.
" Remove BREAK before transport to production!

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you trace the exact Factory pattern execution path in the debugger?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main factory method entry par ek breakpoint set karta hoon aur CASE ya config table lookup watch karta hoon. Main step through karta hoon yeh dekhne ke liye ki kaunsi branch execute hoti hai. Object creation ke baad, main debugger mein returning reference par hover karta hoon aur Object Details use karta hoon yeh confirm karne ke liye ki runtime type expected concrete class se match karta hai. Agar wrong class banayi gayi thi, mujhe immediately pata chal jaata hai ki issue CASE condition logic mein hai ya config table data mein. Yeh bug ko bina guess kiye code ya configuration tak narrow karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I set a breakpoint at the factory method entry and watch the CASE or config table lookup. I step through to see which branch executes. After object creation, I hover over the returning reference in the debugger and use Object Details to confirm the runtime type matches the expected concrete class. If a wrong class was created, I immediately know whether the issue is in the CASE condition logic or the config table data. This narrows the bug to either code or configuration without guessing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Dynamic class instantiation CREATE OBJECT TYPE (lv_class) debug karna: lv_class variable ki value dekho pehle — correct class name hai? Phir step into — agar wrong name hai toh config table issue, agar instantiation fails toh class nahi mili ya constructor error.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Factory return ke baad seedha caller mein jaana bina runtime type verify kiye. Agar wrong class return hui aur tum caller mein debug karo — wrong implementation ka behavior dekh rahe ho, not the factory bug.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Add diagnostic logging in factory for debugging
METHOD create.
  SELECT SINGLE class_name FROM zprice_config
    WHERE bukrs = @iv_bukrs INTO @DATA(lv_class).
  " Debug checkpoint: log what will be instantiated
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    " Config missing - this is the bug location
    RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_factory_config_missing.
  ENDIF.
  CREATE OBJECT ro_strat TYPE (lv_class).
  " Verify: log actual runtime type created
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you identify which Observer received an event and which did not?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main subject ke notify method mein ek breakpoint set karta hoon aur mt_observers table debugger mein inspect karta hoon yeh dekhne ke liye ki saare registered observers aur unke runtime types kya hain. Main LOOP ke through step karta hoon, har observer ka on_event call watch karte hue. Agar loop ek exception ki wajah se early terminate hua, debugger Call Stack dikhata hai kahan exception raise hua. Non-deterministic issues ke liye, main har observer call se pehle aur baad mein temporary SLG1 application log entries add karta hoon — yeh ek audit trail banata hai jo exactly dikhata hai ki kaunse observers execute huye aur kaunse fail huye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I set a breakpoint in the subject's notify method and inspect the mt_observers table in the debugger to see all registered observers and their runtime types. I step through the LOOP, watching each observer's on_event call. If the loop terminated early due to an exception, the debugger Call Stack shows where the exception was raised. For non-deterministic issues, I add temporary SLG1 application log entries before and after each observer call — this creates an audit trail showing exactly which observers executed and which failed.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Application log approach is better than breakpoints for batch Observer issues — batch jobs cannot be paused with interactive debugger. SLG1 entries survive even after job completion and can be analyzed post-mortem.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Interactive debugger use karna for batch Observer issues — batch jobs run in background work process, interactive debugger is ineffective. Use application log for batch debugging.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Observer audit log for debugging
METHOD notify_all.
  LOOP AT mt_observers ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<obs>).
    DATA(lv_name) = cl_abap_classdescr=>describe_by_object_ref(
      p_object_ref = <obs> )->get_relative_name( ).
    TRY.
      <obs>->on_event( iv_event = iv_event_type ).
      " Log success
    CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_err).
      " Log failure with observer name + error
    ENDTRY.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the role of ST05 SQL trace in debugging OOABAP issues?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ST05 SQL trace DB-backed OOABAP patterns debug karne ke liye essential hai. DB reads karne wali repository classes — main ST05 activate karta hoon, issue reproduce karta hoon, deactivate karta hoon, phir analyze karta hoon. Trace har class method se executed exact SQL, table hit counts, execution time, aur table indexes use huye ya nahi dikhata hai. Un factory issues ke liye jahan config table lookup ne wrong data return kiya, ST05 exact SELECT aur uska result set dikhata hai. Yeh confirm karta hai ki bug SQL logic mein hai ya returned data mein.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ST05 SQL trace is essential for debugging DB-backed OOABAP patterns. Repository classes doing DB reads — I activate ST05, reproduce the issue, deactivate, then analyze. The trace shows exact SQL executed from each class method, table hit counts, execution time, and whether table indexes were used. For factory issues where the config table lookup returned wrong data, ST05 shows the exact SELECT and its result set. This confirms whether the bug is in the SQL logic or the returned data.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: ST05 trace filtering: filter by program name or table name in the trace results. OOABAP programs call many tables — filter karo relevant class ke DB calls sirf dekho. Duration column sort karo — slowest queries pehle dikhenge.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: ST05 activate karna aur bahut zyada transactions run karne ke baad analyze karna — trace bahut bada ho jaata hai, relevant entries dhundhna mushkil. Activate karo, targeted reproduction karo, immediately deactivate karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Repository class with traceable DB call
CLASS zcl_price_repository IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_config.
    " ST05 will show this SELECT in trace
    SELECT SINGLE bukrs, class_name, valid_from
      FROM zprice_config
      WHERE bukrs    = @iv_bukrs
        AND active   = 'X'
        AND valid_from <= @sy-datum
      ORDER BY valid_from DESCENDING
      INTO @rs_config.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_config_not_found.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use DESCRIBE OBJECT to debug runtime polymorphism?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: DESCRIBE OBJECT ya CL_ABAP_CLASSDESCR=>DESCRIBE_BY_OBJECT_REF kisi bhi object reference ka runtime class name deta hai. Main isse do contexts mein use karta hoon: investigation ke dauraan actual runtime type ko log ya message mein write karne ke liye temporary debug logging, aur error messages mein include karne ke liye ki kaunsi implementation failure cause ki. Debugger mein, same information Object Details ke through bina code likhe available hoti hai. Production code mein, IF-ELSE mein class names use karne se bachein kyunki yeh polymorphism defeat karta hai — yeh sirf diagnostic purposes ke liye hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: DESCRIBE OBJECT or CL_ABAP_CLASSDESCR=>DESCRIBE_BY_OBJECT_REF gives the runtime class name of any object reference. I use this in two contexts: temporary debug logging to write the actual runtime type to a log or message during investigation, and in error messages to include which implementation caused the failure. In the debugger, the same information is available via Object Details without writing code. In production code, avoid using class names in IF-ELSE as this defeats polymorphism — it is for diagnostic purposes only.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: CL_ABAP_TYPEDESCR hierarchy samjho: DESCRIBE_BY_OBJECT_REF returns CL_ABAP_CLASSDESCR. GET_RELATIVE_NAME gives simple class name. GET_ABSOLUTE_NAME gives package-qualified name. GET_SUPER_CLASS gives parent class. Useful in deep debugging sessions.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Production code mein DESCRIBE OBJECT result se IF-ELSE branch karna — ye polymorphism ko destroy karta hai. Sirf diagnostic aur logging ke liye use karo, never for business logic branching.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Get runtime class name for diagnostic logging
METHOD log_strategy_type.
  DATA(lo_descr) = CAST cl_abap_classdescr(
    cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_object_ref(
      p_object_ref = io_strategy ) ).
  DATA(lv_class_name) = lo_descr->get_relative_name( ).
  MESSAGE |Active strategy: { lv_class_name }| TYPE 'I'.
  " Use only for diagnostics — never for IF/CASE branching!
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you debug a Singleton that appears to return wrong data?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Singleton wrong data debugging: pehle confirm karo ki yeh actually singleton ki tarah behave kar raha hai — do alag locations se get_instance call karo aur debugger mein object references compare karo yeh verify karne ke liye ki yeh same instance par point karte hain. Phir CLASS-DATA attributes inspect karo cached state check karne ke liye. Agar state wrong hai, debugger mein CLASS-DATA variable par ek watchpoint set karo — isme koi bhi write debugger ko automatically trigger karega. Watchpoint hit par call stack reveal karta hai ki kaunsi method incorrectly shared state modify kar rahi hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Singleton wrong data debugging: first confirm it is actually behaving as a singleton — call get_instance from two different locations and compare the object references in the debugger to verify they point to the same instance. Then inspect the CLASS-DATA attributes to check the cached state. If state is wrong, set a watchpoint on the CLASS-DATA variable in the debugger — any write to it will trigger the debugger automatically. The call stack at the watchpoint hit reveals which method is incorrectly modifying the shared state.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Watchpoint is the most powerful tool for singleton state corruption — you do not need to know which method is corrupting, the watchpoint finds it for you. In Eclipse ABAP debugger: Breakpoints tab > Watchpoint > specify class and attribute name.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Manually adding breakpoints to every method that could modify the singleton — tedious and may miss the culprit. Watchpoint is targeted and automatic — use it.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Singleton state audit method (diagnostic use)
CLASS zcl_config_singleton IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD dump_state.  " Call from debugger manually
    LOOP AT mt_cache ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<entry>).
      MESSAGE |Cache: { <entry>-bukrs } = { <entry>-value }|
              TYPE 'I'.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
  " Watchpoint: set on mt_cache in Eclipse debugger
  " Any write to mt_cache will pause execution
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you debug constructor injection issues in OOABAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Constructor injection debugging: constructor mein ek breakpoint set karo aur saare incoming parameters inspect karo. Agar koi bhi injected dependency initial hai, caller tak trace karke dekho ki usne kya pass kiya. Agar caller ko bhi yeh inject hua tha, chain mein break aur upar ho sakta hai — outermost factory ya program se poori object creation chain trace karo. Best practice: har mandatory dependency ke liye constructor ke start par ASSERT IS BOUND checks add karo — yeh failure ko immediate aur explicit banata hai ek clear message ke saath, baad mein ek cryptic null reference dump ke bajaye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Constructor injection debugging: set a breakpoint in the constructor and inspect all incoming parameters. If any injected dependency is initial, trace back to the caller to see what it passed. If the caller also got it injected, the break in the chain could be further up — trace the entire object creation chain from the outermost factory or program. Best practice: add ASSERT IS BOUND checks at the start of the constructor for each mandatory dependency — this makes the failure immediate and explicit with a clear message rather than a cryptic null reference dump later.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Constructor injection chain debugging tip: start from the outermost program or report — ye jaana ki objects kaise wire hue hain. Agar DI container (ABAP DI frameworks jaise abap-di) use ho raha hai toh container configuration check karo.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Dependency null check constructor ke baad karna — agar null dependency hai toh class create hogi normally lekin pehla method call pe dump aayega. Check in constructor for immediate fail-fast behavior.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Fail-fast constructor with dependency validation
CLASS zcl_order_service IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD constructor.
    " Immediate validation of all injected dependencies
    IF io_db IS NOT BOUND.
      RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_invalid_constructor
        EXPORTING iv_message = 'DB repository not injected'.
    ENDIF.
    IF io_logger IS NOT BOUND.
      RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_invalid_constructor
        EXPORTING iv_message = 'Logger not injected'.
    ENDIF.
    mo_db     = io_db.
    mo_logger = io_logger.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the role of unit tests in debugging OOABAP issues?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab ek bug report hota hai, main pehle ek failing unit test likhta hoon jo exactly failure scenario reproduce karta hai. Yeh confirm karta hai ki main bug ko correctly samajhta hoon aur ek repeatable reproduction banata hai. Phir main code fix karta hoon aur verify karta hoon ki test green ho jaata hai. Test codebase mein permanent regression guard ke roop mein rehta hai. OOABAP pattern bugs ke liye, iska matlab ek test likhna jo factory ko problematic input ke saath create kare, ya problematic observer register kare, ya singleton state corruption sequence trigger kare — aur expected correct behavior assert kare.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a bug is reported, I first write a failing unit test that reproduces the exact failure scenario. This confirms I understand the bug correctly and creates a repeatable reproduction. Then I fix the code and verify the test turns green. The test remains in the codebase as a permanent regression guard. For OOABAP pattern bugs, this means writing a test that creates the factory with the problematic input, or registers the problematic observer, or triggers the singleton state corruption sequence — and asserts the expected correct behavior.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Test-driven bug fixing ke fayde: (1) Bug ka clear understanding hota hai before fixing. (2) Fix verification automatic hoti hai. (3) Future protection permanent hoti hai. (4) Bug report + test = complete documentation of the issue and fix.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Fix karna pehle, phir test likhna — this order makes you less confident about the test's validity. If you write the test after fixing, you cannot see it fail, so you cannot be sure it would catch a regression.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Test written BEFORE fix to reproduce the bug
CLASS ltc_factory_bug DEFINITION FOR TESTING
  RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.
  METHODS test_new_company_code_raises_proper_error FOR TESTING.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS ltc_factory_bug IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD test_new_company_code_raises_proper_error.
    TRY.
      zcl_pricing_factory=>create( iv_bukrs = 'NEW1' ).
      cl_abap_unit_assert=>fail( 'Expected exception not raised' ).
    CATCH zcx_factory_config_missing.
      " Correct: exception expected
      cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_true( abap_true ).
    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

ooabap-029.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: OOABAP aur Design Patterns mein performance problems tab aate hain jab OO ka flexibility overuse ho jaata hai ya galat jagah use ho. Kuch examples: Factory pattern mein har baar naya object create ho raha hai even jab same object reuse kiya ja sakta tha — ye memory aur initialization overhead banata hai. Observer pattern mein 50 observers registered hain, ek event aaya aur 50 observer calls hue — har call ek DB operation karta hai, toh 50 DB operations ek event pe. Strategy pattern mein har calculation ke liye naya strategy object create ho raha hai instead of reusing existing objects. Deep inheritance chain (6-7 levels) mein method resolution slow hoti hai. Interface dispatching generally fast hota hai lekin agar ek interface method ke andar heavy logic hai aur woh bahut baar call ho toh cumulatively slow ho sakta hai. Aur sabse common: Repository class mein SELECT inside a loop — OO architecture ne loop ko class ke andar chhupa diya lekin N+1 query problem wahi hai jo procedural mein hoti thi. Performance mein OO ka rule: design flexibility ke liye, performance ke liye premature optimization avoid karo — but jaano kahan bottleneck ho sakta hai.
ooabap-029.hinglishMasterExplanation:
OOABAP performance problems detail mein samjho — category-wise:

Category 1 — Object Creation Overhead:
New operator har baar ek heap allocation karta hai. Agar ek processing loop mein 10,000 records hain aur har record ke liye NEW zcl_pricing_engine( ) karo — 10,000 allocations, 10,000 constructor calls. Fix: Strategy aur Factory patterns mein object reuse karo — ek baar create, baar baar use. Object pool pattern consider karo for expensive objects. Memory impact: large objects with big internal tables as attributes — har instance apna data carry karta hai. OOABAP mein memory usage profile karo with transaction SM50 or ABAP Memory Inspector.

Category 2 — Observer Pattern N-Observer Problem:
Ek event pe bahut zyada observers notify hone chahiye. Agar observers asynchronous hote toh issue nahi — lekin synchronous observers ek ke baad ek execute hote hain, total time = sum of all observer times. Ek observer jo heavy SELECT karta hai — poori chain slow ho jaati hai. Fix: heavy observers ko asynchronous banao (CALL FUNCTION IN BACKGROUND TASK). Light observers synchronous rakho. Observer priority define karo — critical observers pehle.

Category 3 — Repository N+1 Query Problem:
OO architecture mein repository class LOOP ke andar SELECT kar sakti hai. Example: zcl_order_repository=>get_order( ) ek SELECT karta hai per call. Calling code mein loop hai: LOOP AT lt_order_numbers > get_order( ) > N selects. Classic N+1 problem OO wrapper ke peeche chhupi hai. Fix: Repository mein bulk method banao — get_orders( lt_order_numbers ) jo ek SELECT FOR ALL ENTRIES ya IN karta hai.

Category 4 — Deep Inheritance Resolution:
Super->method( ) calls chain bana sakte hain — zcl_level5 > zcl_level4 > zcl_level3 > zcl_level2 > zcl_base. Har level pe method lookup hota hai. Generally fast hai ABAP mein (compiled) lekin 8+ level deep inheritance mein noticeable ho sakta hai. Fix: composition over deep inheritance prefer karo.

Category 5 — Singleton Cache Mismanagement:
Singleton cache bahut badi ho jaati hai aur kabhi clear nahi hoti — memory leak effect. Ya singleton cache stale data se kaam karta hai performance ke liye lekin business data wrong dikhata hai. Cache invalidation strategy define karo — TTL (time to live) ya event-based invalidation.
ooabap-029.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Performance issues in OOABAP arise in five main areas. First, excessive object creation — creating new instances per record in processing loops instead of reusing strategy objects causes significant heap allocation and GC overhead. Fix: create once, reuse across the loop. Second, Observer N-observer problem — synchronous notification of many heavy observers per event; fix is to make heavy observers asynchronous using background function modules. Third, hidden N+1 queries in repository classes — an OO wrapper hides a SELECT inside a method called per iteration; fix is bulk repository methods using FOR ALL ENTRIES. Fourth, deep inheritance chains causing method resolution overhead — prefer composition. Fifth, singleton cache growing unbounded — implement TTL or event-based cache invalidation. I profile with SAT transaction for CPU hotspots and ST05 for SQL bottlenecks.'

Hinglish tip: Performance interview mein hamesha tool ka naam lo — SAT, ST05. Aur specific pattern bolo — 'N+1 query behind repository', 'synchronous observer chain'. Ye technical depth aur real experience show karta hai.
ooabap-029.codeExamples:
" Performance problem: New object per iteration (BAD)
LOOP AT lt_materials ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<mat>).
  " 10,000 new objects created and destroyed
  DATA(lo_calc) = NEW zcl_pricing_engine( iv_bukrs = lv_bukrs ).
  <mat>-price = lo_calc->calculate( <mat>-matnr ).
ENDLOOP.

" Performance fix: Create once, reuse (GOOD)
DATA(lo_calc) = NEW zcl_pricing_engine( iv_bukrs = lv_bukrs ).
LOOP AT lt_materials ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<mat>).
  <mat>-price = lo_calc->calculate( <mat>-matnr ).
ENDLOOP.

" N+1 fix: Bulk repository method
CLASS zcl_order_repo IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_orders_bulk.  " One SELECT for all keys
    IF it_vbeln IS INITIAL. RETURN. ENDIF.
    SELECT vbeln, netwr, erdat
      FROM vbak
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @it_vbeln
      WHERE vbeln = @it_vbeln-vbeln
      INTO TABLE @rt_orders.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
ooabap-029.interviewScriptEnglish: Performance issues in OOABAP arise in five main areas. First, excessive object creation — creating new instances per record in processing loops instead of reusing strategy objects causes significant heap allocation and GC overhead. Fix: create once, reuse across the loop. Second, Observer N-observer problem — synchronous notification of many heavy observers per event; fix is to make heavy observers asynchronous using background function modules. Third, hidden N+1 queries in repository classes — an OO wrapper hides a SELECT inside a method called per iteration; fix is bulk repository methods using FOR ALL ENTRIES. Fourth, deep inheritance chains causing method resolution overhead — prefer composition. Fifth, singleton cache growing unbounded — implement TTL or event-based cache invalidation. I profile with SAT transaction for CPU hotspots and ST05 for SQL bottlenecks.
ooabap-029.interviewScriptHinglish: OOABAP mein performance issues paanch main areas mein arise hote hain. Pehla, excessive object creation — processing loops mein per record new instances banana, strategy objects reuse karne ki jagah, significant heap allocation aur GC overhead karta hai. Fix: ek baar banao, loop ke across reuse karo. Doosra, Observer N-observer problem — per event many heavy observers ka synchronous notification; fix yeh hai ki heavy observers ko background function modules use karke asynchronous banao. Teesra, repository classes mein hidden N+1 queries — ek OO wrapper SELECT ko method ke andar hide karta hai jo per iteration call hota hai; fix bulk repository methods hain FOR ALL ENTRIES ke saath. Chautha, deep inheritance chains method resolution overhead cause karte hain — composition prefer karo. Paancha, singleton cache unbounded grow karta hai — TTL ya event-based cache invalidation implement karo. Main CPU hotspots ke liye SAT transaction se profile karta hoon aur SQL bottlenecks ke liye ST05 se.
